vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1527

plan.title: scope:complete updates PROJECT-DEFINITION references but leaves registry item status stale

narratives.Description: Completing a scope updates PROJECT-DEFINITION references but can leave the matching plan.items registry entry proposed. This story makes registry status synchronization deterministic or explicitly validated so project state does not contradict itself.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

In a vBRIEF-centric consumer project, `task scope:complete -- <active-scope-vbrief>` moves the scope file to `vbrief/completed/` and updates the `PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json` reference URI to point at `completed/...`, but the matching `plan.items[]` registry entry can remain at `status: proposed`.

## Observed behavior

After completing a scope with:

```bash
task scope:complete -- vbrief/active/2026-06-04-sites-membership-api.vbrief.json
```

The lifecycle move succeeded and `PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json` references now include:

```json
{
  "uri": "completed/2026-06-04-sites-membership-api.vbrief.json",
  "type": "x-vbrief/plan",
  "title": "Sites and Site Membership API"
}
```

But the corresponding registry item still reads:

```json
{
  "id": "sites-membership-api",
  "title": "Sites and Site Membership API",
  "status": "proposed"
}
```

The same consumer project also had previously completed scope references whose registry items still showed `proposed`, suggesting this is not isolated to one vBRIEF.

## Expected behavior

When lifecycle tooling completes a scope and updates `PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json`, the matching `plan.items[]` entry should either:

- be updated to `status: completed`, or
- be intentionally left alone with a documented validation rule explaining that `plan.items[].status` is not authoritative and may diverge from lifecycle folder/status.

## Impact

Agents and operators can see contradictory project state in the same file: references point at completed scope vBRIEFs, but the scope registry still appears proposed. This makes work selection and dependency reasoning more error-prone unless agents explicitly prefer lifecycle folders over `PROJECT-DEFINITION.plan.items`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `task scope:complete` has deterministic behavior for the matching PROJECT-DEFINITION registry item.
- Either the registry status is synchronized, or docs/validation make the intentional divergence explicit.
- `task vbrief:validate` or a related guard catches unintended stale registry statuses if synchronization is the intended contract.

## Notes

This report is based on consumer-side behavior observed in a vBRIEF v0.6 project using Deft v0.41.0. No project-specific implementation details are needed to reproduce beyond having a PROJECT-DEFINITION registry item matching the completed scope id.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Update scripts/scope_lifecycle.py and the PROJECT-DEFINITION helper path so scope completion either synchronizes the matching plan.items[] status to completed or documents/validates an intentional divergence.
2. Add focused regression tests in tests/cli/test_scope_lifecycle.py and project/validation tests that complete a scope and assert the PROJECT-DEFINITION registry state is deterministic.
narratives.UserStory: As a Deft operator, I want project registry status to agree with completed scope references, so that work selection does not see contradictory lifecycle state.
narratives.Traces: Issue #1527; scripts/scope_lifecycle.py PROJECT-DEFINITION reference maintenance; task vbrief:validate.

items.title: Scope completion has a registry contract

narrative.Acceptance: Given a scope vBRIEF is completed, when PROJECT-DEFINITION contains a matching registry item, then the item status is deterministically synchronized or an explicit validation/docs contract explains divergence.
narrative.Traces: Issue #1527; scripts/scope_lifecycle.py PROJECT-DEFINITION reference maintenance; task vbrief:validate.

items.title: References and registry do not contradict unintentionally

narrative.Acceptance: Given PROJECT-DEFINITION references point at completed scope vBRIEFs, when validation or project rendering runs, then stale proposed registry statuses are caught or corrected according to the selected contract.
narrative.Traces: Issue #1527; scripts/scope_lifecycle.py PROJECT-DEFINITION reference maintenance; task vbrief:validate.

items.title: Regression tests cover consumer project shape

narrative.Acceptance: Given a consumer-like vBRIEF project fixture, when task scope:complete runs, then tests assert the registry item outcome and reference rewrite together.
narrative.Traces: Issue #1527; scripts/scope_lifecycle.py PROJECT-DEFINITION reference maintenance; task vbrief:validate.

references.title: Issue #1527: scope:complete updates PROJECT-DEFINITION references but leaves registry item status stale

verify_commands: uv run pytest tests/cli/test_scope_lifecycle.py tests/cli/test_project_render.py tests/cli/test_vbrief_validate.py
expected_outputs: scope lifecycle and project validation tests assert deterministic PROJECT-DEFINITION registry status behavior
